L2.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in an image decoding routine where a multiplication of image dimension parameters derived from a file header is used to compute a memory reallocation size without first checking for overflow. A crafted image file with very large dimension values can cause the multiplication to wrap around, leading to a smaller-than-expected buffer allocation followed by an out-of-bounds write.

L3.area: TIFF image codec and decoding routines
L3.description: An integer overflow vulnerability exists in the TIFF image decoding logic where a multiplication of image dimension parameters derived from the TIFF file header is used to compute a memory reallocation size without first checking for overflow. A crafted TIFF file with very large dimension values can cause the multiplication to wrap around, leading to a smaller-than-expected buffer allocation followed by an out-of-bounds write.

ground_truth.reason: The TIFF decoding function multiplies image dimension parameters (tile dimensions or rows-per-strip) by the row byte size to compute a reallocation size without checking for integer overflow, allowing a crafted TIFF to trigger a heap buffer overflow via a wrapped allocation size.
